---
title: "Exercise 04 — Nextflow testing with nf-test"
format: html
toc: true
---

::: {.callout-note}
🇫🇷 [Version française](../../../exercises/04_nextflow/README.qmd)
:::

## Objective

Write nf-test tests for the `COUNT_LINES` process and the `PROCESS_TABLE` workflow.

## Context

The example pipeline (`pipelines/minimal/`) contains:

- `modules/count_lines.nf` — counts lines in a TSV, produces a `.count` file and a `.summary.txt`
- `modules/summarise.nf` — computes mean/n per group, produces a `.stats.tsv`
- `workflows/process_table.nf` — chains COUNT_LINES → SUMMARISE

::: {.callout-note}
**This pipeline is a pedagogical simulation.**

The modules use bash scripts (awk, wc). In production, you would test **observable properties** of a real tool (line count, output format, exit code). The testing logic is identical — the distinction matters for understanding what your assertions actually prove.
:::

## Data

```
training/data/records.tsv   # 20 rows (id, group, value)
training/data/empty.tsv     # Header only, no data
```

## Exercise — Part 1: process test

Complete `training/tests/nf-test/count_lines.nf.test`:

```groovy
nextflow_process {
    name "Test COUNT_LINES"
    script "../../pipelines/minimal/modules/count_lines.nf"
    process "COUNT_LINES"

    test("TSV valide → count produit") {
        when {
            process {
                """
                input[0] = [
                    // TODO : définir le meta et le fichier d'entrée
                ]
                """
            }
        }
        then {
            // TODO : vérifier que le process a réussi
            // TODO : vérifier que count == 20
            // TODO : vérifier le snapshot
        }
    }

    test("fichier vide → process échoue") {
        when {
            process {
                """
                input[0] = [
                    // TODO
                ]
                """
            }
        }
        then {
            // TODO : vérifier que le process a échoué
        }
    }
}
```

## Exercise — Part 2: workflow test

Complete `training/tests/nf-test/process_table.nf.test`:

```groovy
nextflow_workflow {
    name "Test PROCESS_TABLE"
    script "../../pipelines/minimal/workflows/process_table.nf"
    workflow "PROCESS_TABLE"

    test("TSV valide → count et stats produits") {
        when {
            workflow {
                """
                input[0] = Channel.of([
                    // TODO
                ])
                """
            }
        }
        then {
            // TODO : vérifier succès
            // TODO : vérifier que count.size() == 1, summary.size() == 1, stats.size() == 1
            // TODO : vérifier une valeur comportementale (ex : count == 20)
            // TODO : snapshot
        }
    }
}
```

## Running the tests

```bash
cd training/tests/nf-test
nf-test test count_lines.nf.test --verbose
nf-test test --verbose   # tous les tests
```

## Expected result

```
Test COUNT_LINES
  ✔ TSV valide → count et summary produits
  ✔ fichier vide (en-tête seul) → process échoue

Test PROCESS_TABLE workflow
  ✔ TSV valide → count, summary et stats produits

SUCCESS: Executed 3 tests
```

## Bonus — Testing the full pipeline

If you have completed both parts, add a `nextflow_pipeline` test on `main.nf`:

```groovy
nextflow_pipeline {
    name "Test pipeline complet"
    script "../../pipelines/minimal/main.nf"

    test("pipeline complet — sorties présentes") {
        when {
            params {
                input  = "${projectDir}/data/records.tsv"
                outdir = "${outputDir}"
            }
        }
        then {
            // TODO : vérifier workflow.success
            // TODO : vérifier le nombre de tâches avec workflow.trace.succeeded()
            // TODO : vérifier qu'un fichier de sortie existe et contient la valeur attendue
        }
    }
}
```

## Solution

→ See [solution.qmd](solution.qmd)
